codeReview.summary: Reviewed this fix rerun against the prior failure in storm-to-lead-pipeline. The implementation now routes counties through county-correct sources (Miami-Dade -> parcel GeoProp, Broward -> county permit scraper, Palm Beach -> explicit skip because no public endpoint) and applies area specificity by narrowing Miami-Dade scans to candidate zip and passing candidate city into Broward permit scanning. This resolves the two original blocking issues around non-Miami routing and county-wide-only targeting.

issues.description: `test_city_specificity_used_for_broward_area_scan` does not assert that `city='Deerfield Beach'` was actually forwarded (or that non-matching cities are excluded); it only checks that `mock_scrape` was called and `call_args` is non-null. This weakens regression protection for the city-targeting contract even though current implementation does pass `city`.

summary: Pass. The fix commit db3166f adequately addresses the prior blocking failures: non-Miami candidates are no longer routed through the Miami-only parcel endpoint, and area specificity is now used (zip narrowing for Miami-Dade and city filter flow for Broward).
